description: Integrate with the WordPress REST API (wp-json) to enable one-click publishing of blog posts to self-hosted WordPress sites and WordPress.com. Users connect via application password, then publish posts with categories, tags, featured images, and SEO metadata. Supports both draft and published states.

phases.name: Database Schema
phases.description: Add wordpressConnections table and wordpressPublishedUrl column to posts, then run migration

subtasks.title: Add wordpressConnections table to schema
subtasks.description: Add a new pgTable 'wordpress_connections' to packages/db/src/schema.ts with fields: id, workspaceId (FK to workspaces, cascade delete), siteUrl, username, encryptedAppPassword, isActive boolean default true, createdAt, updatedAt. Add wordpressConnections to workspacesRelations (many). Add wordpressConnectionsRelations pointing back to workspace.

subtasks.title: Add wordpressPublishedUrl column to posts table
subtasks.description: Add nullable text column 'wordpress_published_url' to the posts table in packages/db/src/schema.ts so we can store the WordPress post URL after publishing. Also add wordpressPostId integer column to store the WP post ID.

subtasks.title: Run DB migration (generate and push)
subtasks.description: Run 'bun run db:generate' then 'bun run db:push' from the repo root to apply schema changes to the Neon PostgreSQL database. Verify migration succeeds without errors.

phases.name: WordPress API Client Library
phases.description: Create reusable WordPress REST API client and password encryption utilities

subtasks.title: Create password encryption utilities
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/lib/wordpress/crypto.ts with encryptAppPassword(plaintext: string): string and decryptAppPassword(encrypted: string): string using Node.js crypto module (AES-256-CBC) with BETTER_AUTH_SECRET as the key (hashed to 32 bytes via SHA-256). Store as 'iv:ciphertext' hex string.

subtasks.title: Create WordPress REST API client
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/lib/wordpress/client.ts with a WordPressClient class: constructor(siteUrl, username, appPassword), getApiBase() normalizes URL to ensure it ends with /wp-json/wp/v2, testConnection() calls /users/me with Basic auth and returns site info, getCategories() and getTags() for listing, createPost(params: { title, htmlContent, excerpt, categories, tags, featuredMediaId, status }) POSTs to /posts. All methods throw descriptive errors on failure. Handles both WordPress.com (wordpress.com/wp-json) and self-hosted sites.

phases.name: Backend API Routes
phases.description: Create API routes for managing WordPress connections and publishing posts

subtasks.title: Create WordPress connection CRUD route
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/app/api/workspace/[slug]/wordpress/route.ts with: GET (fetch connection for workspace, returns { connected, siteUrl, username } without password), POST (save connection: body has siteUrl, username, appPassword — encrypt password with crypto.ts before inserting/upserting to wordpressConnections), DELETE (set isActive=false or hard delete). Follow style/route.ts pattern for auth/ownership.

subtasks.title: Create WordPress connection test route
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/app/api/workspace/[slug]/wordpress/test/route.ts with POST handler: (1) fetch stored connection, (2) decrypt app password, (3) instantiate WordPressClient and call testConnection(), (4) also fetch categories and tags, (5) return { success: true, siteTitle, wpVersion, categories: [{id, name}], tags: [{id, name}] } or { success: false, error: string }.

subtasks.title: Create WordPress publish route
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/app/api/content/[id]/wordpress/publish/route.ts with POST handler: (1) verify post ownership via workspace join, (2) fetch workspace WordPress connection, (3) decrypt app password, (4) convert post markdown to HTML using markdownToHtml() from @/lib/export, (5) call WordPressClient.createPost() with { title, htmlContent, excerpt, categories, tags, featuredMediaId, status: 'draft'|'publish' }, (6) update posts row with wordpressPublishedUrl and wordpressPostId, (7) return { success, wordpressPostId, wordpressUrl }.

phases.name: WordPress Settings UI
phases.description: Add WordPress connection management page in workspace settings

subtasks.title: Create WordPress settings page
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/[workspace]/settings/wordpress/page.tsx as a 'use client' component using useParams, useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient. Show: (1) section header 'WordPress Integration', (2) if connected: green status indicator with site URL, username, Test Connection button, Disconnect button; (3) if not connected: form with Site URL input (placeholder 'https://myblog.com'), Username input, Application Password input (type=password), Connect button. On connect success: invalidate query. On test: show modal/alert with site title and categories/tags list. Follow api-keys/page.tsx pattern with sf-* Tailwind classes.

subtasks.title: Add WordPress link to settings navigation
subtasks.description: Find the settings layout/navigation (check apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/[workspace]/settings/layout.tsx). Add a WordPress nav item with Globe icon linking to /[workspace]/settings/wordpress. If no layout.tsx exists, add link directly to settings/page.tsx.

phases.name: Publish UI Components
phases.description: Add Publish to WordPress option to the content editor with modal for configuring publish settings

subtasks.title: Add useWordPressConnection and useWordPressPublish hooks
subtasks.description: Add to apps/dashboard/src/hooks/use-content.ts: (1) useWordPressConnection(workspace: string) — useQuery fetching GET /api/workspace/[workspace]/wordpress, returns { connected, siteUrl, username } or null; (2) useWordPressPublish() — useMutation posting to /api/content/[id]/wordpress/publish with body { categories, tags, excerpt, featuredImageUrl, wpStatus }. On success, invalidate ['post', id] and ['content'] queries.

subtasks.title: Create WordPressPublishModal component
subtasks.description: Create apps/dashboard/src/components/content/wordpress-publish-modal.tsx — a modal overlay (fixed inset-0 backdrop) with: (1) title 'Publish to WordPress', (2) connection status (site URL if connected, or warning if not), (3) excerpt textarea, (4) categories multi-select (fetched from /wordpress/test endpoint or stored), (5) tags input (comma-separated), (6) featured image URL input, (7) radio: 'Publish immediately' vs 'Save as WordPress draft', (8) 'Publish' button that calls useWordPressPublish(). On success shows WordPress URL link. On error shows error message. Uses sf-* Tailwind classes, X close button.

subtasks.title: Update ExportDropdown to add WordPress publish option
subtasks.description: Update apps/dashboard/src/components/content/export-dropdown.tsx: (1) add props postId: string, workspace: string, (2) call useWordPressConnection(workspace) inside component, (3) add a horizontal divider <hr> after the Download .md item, (4) add a 'Publish to WordPress' button with Globe icon (lucide-react) — disabled with tooltip 'Connect WordPress in Settings' when not connected, (5) clicking opens WordPressPublishModal (manage open state). Import and render WordPressPublishModal conditionally.

subtasks.title: Wire up content editor page with WordPress features
subtasks.description: Update apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/[workspace]/content/[postId]/page.tsx: (1) pass postId={postId} workspace={workspace} to ExportDropdown, (2) if post.data.wordpressPublishedUrl exists, show a 'View on WordPress' link (Globe icon, opens in new tab) in the toolbar near the Save button.

qa_checklist: Connect a WordPress site successfully in Settings > WordPress
Test connection shows site title plus categories and tags list
Publish a blog post to WordPress as 'draft' — verify in WP admin dashboard
Publish a blog post to WordPress as 'published' — verify live URL is accessible
Published WordPress URL appears as 'View on WordPress' link in content editor
Error message shown when WordPress site URL is unreachable
Error message shown when application password is invalid
Settings navigation shows WordPress link with Globe icon
Export dropdown shows WordPress option only when connection is active
Application password is encrypted in DB (no plaintext visible)
